title: Lesson Quiz: Respectful Online Communication & Digital Etiquette
description: Assessment of key concepts aligned to the lesson objective.

questions.prompt: Why is tone important in online communication?

choices.text: Because tone is always clear in written messages
choices.feedback: Tone is often unclear in written communication and can be misunderstood.

choices.text: Because tone can affect how messages are interpreted by others
choices.feedback: Correct. Tone influences how a message is understood and can lead to misunderstandings.

choices.text: Because tone only matters in face-to-face conversations
choices.feedback: Tone is especially important online because nonverbal cues are missing.

choices.text: Because tone does not impact communication
choices.feedback: Tone plays a major role in how messages are received.

questions.prompt: Misunderstandings are more likely in online communication because nonverbal cues are missing.

choices.feedback: Correct. Without facial expressions or tone of voice, messages can be misinterpreted.
Misunderstandings are more common online due to lack of nonverbal cues.

questions.prompt: Which example demonstrates good digital etiquette?

choices.text: Using respectful language when responding to others
choices.feedback: Correct. Respectful language is a key part of digital etiquette.

choices.text: Typing messages in all caps to show urgency
choices.feedback: All caps can be interpreted as shouting and is not considered respectful.
Interrupting is not respectful in digital communication.
Respectful communication includes acknowledging others' perspectives.

questions.prompt: What is one way to reduce misunderstandings in online communication?

choices.text: Respond quickly without reviewing your message
choices.feedback: Reviewing messages helps ensure clarity and tone.

choices.text: Use clear language and reread messages before sending
choices.feedback: Correct. Clarity and review help reduce misunderstandings.

choices.text: Assume others will understand your intent

questions.prompt: Which behavior reflects respectful online communication?

choices.text: Responding with sarcasm that could be misunderstood
choices.feedback: Sarcasm can easily be misinterpreted online.

choices.text: Taking time to consider how your message may be received
choices.feedback: Correct. Thoughtful communication helps maintain respect and clarity.
Tone should always be considered in communication.
